title: Temporary DAO Funding to Preserve Core Lore, Design, and Community Functions
brief: In an effort to boost our runway and ensure Star Atlas can fulfill the roadmap and deliver the releases planned for this year, ATMTA (the leading developer of Star Atlas) was forced to make painful cuts to its talent. Unfortunately, my position as a lore writer and community team member was terminated as part of this process. Despite this, I remain confident in Star Atlas and in the incredible game modes that still have the potential to turn this situation around, such as Holosim, C4, Z.ink, and Siege Mode. This PIP exists because I want to continue fighting alongside this community and contributing to Star Atlas during this critical period.
description:
# 1. Temporary DAO Funding to Preserve Core Lore, Design, and Community Functions
* Author: José - Noot
* Type: treasury
* Date: February 2026

___
 
# 2. SUMMARY

## 2.1 

In an effort to boost our runway and ensure Star Atlas can fulfill the roadmap and deliver the releases planned for this year, **ATMTA** (the leading developer of Star Atlas) was forced to make painful cuts to its talent. Unfortunately, my position as a lore writer and community team member was terminated as part of this process. Despite this, I remain confident in Star Atlas and in the incredible game modes that still have the potential to turn this situation around, such as **Holosim, C4, Z.ink, and Siege Mode**. This PIP exists because I want to continue fighting alongside this community and contributing to Star Atlas during this critical period.

## 2.2

The funds requested are a total of 30,000 usdc paid out over 6 months at 5,000 usd a month and will be taken out of the treasury.
  
# 3. MOTIVATION

As many of you know, I am deeply passionate about Star Atlas. It has been my pleasure and privilege to be in the trenches of this project with all of you for many years, as a lore writer, content writer, community team member, and in many other roles that I held at ATMTA. I debated internally for a long time whether it was right for me to submit this PIP. Ultimately, however, the reality is that I want to be here more than anything, fighting until the end together with the people of this community, no matter what the outcome may be. For that reason, I am submitting this idea to the **Star Atlas Community DAO**. 

---

# 4. SPECIFICATION

## 4.1 Definitions 
* **ATMTA** — The leading developer of Star Atlas, and the company where I have been embedded as a contributor across lore, design, community, and related functions. 
* **DAO** — The Star Atlas Community DAO. 
* **Deliverables** — The concrete outputs ATMTA defines as necessary for me in the exertion of my functions (quest packs, lore entries, documentation drafts, event execution, scripts, and similar work products). 
* **Term** — The fixed engagement duration of six (6) months. 
* **Earmarked funding** — Funds transferred to ATMTA and intended exclusively to cover my reduced salary during the engagement.

## 4.2 Proposal

I propose that the Star Atlas DAO temporarily fund my continued work within ATMTA. Under this structure: 
* I will continue working at ATMTA, fully embedded in the existing team and workflows. 
* The Star Atlas DAO will provide **USDC 5,000 per month to ATMTA**. 
* These funds will be used by ATMTA to cover my reduced salary, allowing the company to retain critical capacity without increasing its current burn.
* This engagement is proposed for a fixed duration of **six (6) months**.

Unfortunately, my personal circumstances do not allow me to work pro bono. This structure allows me to continue contributing meaningfully to Star Atlas while hopefully respecting both DAO funds and ATMTA’s current constraints.

## 4.3 Scope of Work

My work at ATMTA has revolved around the following responsibilities, which I propose to continue fulfilling under this DAO-supported arrangement: 

### 4.3.1 Holosim

* Creating the quest system content
* Developing lore
* Designing the achievement system
* Defining rule systems
* Supporting John with general game design

### 4.3.2 Lore
 
Leading and developing Star Atlas lore across all game modes and content

### 4.3.3 Legal
 
* Reviewing legal documentation
* Creating user terms and conditions
* Supporting legal documentation for products such as Z.ink and Holosim

### 4.3.4 Community 

* Hosting Star Atlas public events, including: Star Atlas Brew, Econ Forum, Iris Blessing, Golden Carnival, Lore Keepers, Game Nights, and the Brazilian Brew
* Supporting the moderation team and the broader community
* Sharing penguin love

### 4.3.5 UE5 / Project Galia 
* Direct co-creation with Brad on Project Galia
* Supporting UE5 initiatives with lore, scripts, and game design
* Providing general lore guidance and content across all UE5 projects

### 4.3.6 Implementation (Functional & Non-Technical)
* ATMTA will establish the **deliverables necessary for me in the exertion of my functions**, aligned with internal priorities and workflows.
* ATMTA will track whether these deliverables are being fulfilled as expected, using the same internal accountability that applies to any contributor embedded in the team.
* If I fail to fulfill the functions and deliverables necessary, **ATMTA will report this to the DAO** so the community has visibility and can respond accordingly.
* The DAO funding remains strictly time-bound and tied to this six-month period, with no automatic renewal.

### 4.3.7 New Processes / Systems
* A simple accountability loop where **ATMTA defines deliverables**, and **reports to the DAO** if those deliverables are not being met.
* No new tooling, hosting, or technical systems are required.
___

# 5. STAKEHOLDER IMPACT

## 5.1 Stakeholders 

* **Star Atlas community / DAO tokenholders**
* **ATMTA teams** (Holosim, UE5, lore, community, legal/product documentation support) 
* **Moderation team and community event participants**
* **Players and broader ecosystem contributors**

For New Players: 
* A compelling entry point into the world of Star Atlas. 
* Clearer connection to lore, factions, environments, and themes. 

For the DAO & Ecosystem: 
* Enhanced visibility and shareable content for social platforms. 
* Strengthened brand identity and worldbuilding. 
* Long-term narrative infrastructure supports player retention and marketing efforts. 

## 5.2 Positive impacts 
* Preserves continuity in core lore, quest content, and community operations during a difficult period. 
* Keeps me embedded inside ATMTA rather than creating an external contractor workflow, which minimizes overhead and delays. 
* Allows ATMTA to retain critical capacity without increasing burn. 
* Creates a clear accountability path: deliverables are set by ATMTA, and non-performance is reported to the DAO.

## 5.3 Negative impacts

* DAO treasury spends **USDC 30,000 total** across six months that could otherwise support other initiatives.
* Some community members may prefer direct contributor funding rather than funding routed to ATMTA.
* The DAO may be concerned about precedent; this is being proposed as a one-time exception due to unusual circumstances.


---

# 6. IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS

## 6.1 Plan for execution (chronological): 
* If the PIP is approved, ATMTA will re-engage me into the team and define the deliverables necessary for my functions. 
* The DAO will send **USDC 5,000 per month** to ATMTA, earmarked to cover my reduced salary.
* I will perform my work fully embedded within ATMTA’s existing workflows.
* ATMTA will monitor performance against the deliverables it defined.
* If I fail to fulfill the functions and deliverables necessary, **ATMTA will report this to the DAO**.
* The engagement ends automatically at the conclusion of the six (6) month term unless a new proposal is submitted and approved.

# 6.2 Parties responsible: 
* **ATMTA:** define deliverables, assign priorities, manage workflow integration, and report to the DAO if deliverables are not met. 
* **José:** execute the work and fulfill the defined functions and deliverables. 
* **DAO:** provide time-bound monthly funding to ATMTA under the terms of this proposal. 


# 6.3 Technical requirements / hosting solution: None. Potential challenges and how they will be addressed:  

The main risk is whether DAO funds are spent without receiving value. This is addressed by:
 
* ATMTA defining deliverables internally, based on real operational needs. 
* ATMTA reporting to the DAO if deliverables are not being fulfilled.
* The fixed six-month duration with no automatic renewal.

___

# 7. TIMELINE 

## 7.1 Expected start date

As soon as practical after PIP approval, once ATMTA completes internal onboarding steps. **Key milestones / phases** — This engagement runs for **six (6) months**, with responsibilities executed continuously across the scope areas listed above. ATMTA will define the deliverables necessary during this period. 


## 7.2 Estimated completion date

Six (6) months after kickoff.

## 7.3 Payment schedule 
* no upfront
* **USDC 5,000 per month** paid by the Star Atlas Community DAO to ATMTA
* Total (6 months): USDC 30,000
* Fixed term, no automatic renewal

## 7.4 Dependencies 

* ATMTA’s ability to re-integrate me into the team and define deliverables tied to actual priorities.
* DAO’s ability to execute the monthly payments as scheduled.


---
# 8. COST & RESOURCES ALLOCATION 

## 8.1 One-Time Costs  
* None requested. 

## 8.2 Ongoing Costs 
* **Monthly Cost:** USDC 5,000
* **Total Cost (6 months):** USDC 30,000 
* **Recipient:** ATMTA (earmarked for contributor compensation) 
* This proposal represents a fixed, time-bound expense with no automatic renewal. 
* **Source of funding:** DAO treasury.
* **Who will manage these ongoing efforts?** ATMTA will manage execution, deliverables definition, and accountability reporting. 


___

# 9. ADDITIONAL DETAILS 

## Conflicts of interest

As biased as I obviously am in this situation, I ask you to vote with a clear head. Please do not vote “yes” out of sympathy for me. This is the DAO’s money, and it should only be spent if you truly believe this is the best possible outcome for Star Atlas. I will not hold any ill feelings toward anyone who votes “no.” 

## Conflict of Interest Disclosure:

I am the direct beneficiary of this proposal and therefore have a clear conflict of interest. I am submitting this PIP transparently and openly, and I will not participate in moderation, interpretation, or governance decisions related to this proposal beyond providing factual clarifications when requested by the community. 

## Earlier versions

None.
